perturbation.original_text: “Term ” means a period of 12 months commencing on the date of this Confidentiality Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: “Term ” means a period of 6 months commencing on the date of this Confidentiality Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: The definition of 'Term' is changed from 12 months to 6 months. This contradicts the statement in Section 8 concerning Non-Solicitation, where it states 'provided that this paragraph 8 shall cease to bind the Recipient on the expiry of the Term'. This contradiction impacts the duration of the non-solicitation clause, halving the intended duration, potentially causing an in-text contradiction when referring the non-solicitation term.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 8. Non -Solicitation: The Recipient will not, and the Recipient will cause its Representatives not to , solicit for hire or employment, directly or indirectly, any officer or employee of the Disclosing Party or its direct and indirect subsidiaries that the Recipient becomes aware of or is in contact with in Red River Oil Inc. Confidentiality Agreement Page 4 of 8 connection with its evaluation of a Transaction. For the purposes of this clause, “solicitation ͟ shall not include solicitation of any officer or employee of the Disclosing Party or its direct and indirect subsidiaries who is solicited: (i) by advertising in a newspaper or periodical of general circulat ion; or (ii) indirectly through a personnel search agency engaged by the Recipient generally (not specifically in respect of the Disclosing Party) provided that the Recipient shall not pursue hiring of any officer or employee of the Disclosing Party or its direct and indirect subsidiaries once the Recipient is advised that such person is an officer or employee of the Disclosing Party ; provided that this paragraph 8 shall cease to bind the Recipient on the expiry of the Term.
perturbation.justification: The change in the definition of 'Term' from 12 months to 6 months directly contradicts Section 8, which states that the non-solicitation clause ceases to bind the recipient on the expiry of the Term. This means the duration of the non-solicitation agreement is effectively halved, creating a clear and direct conflict regarding the intended duration of the non-solicitation clause, and falling under Misaligned Terminology causing an in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: The Recipient will safeguard and strictly control the dissemination of the Evaluati on Material and not release or disclose any Evaluation Material to any Person, other than its Representatives and in each case only those Representatives who need to receive such information in connection with its Evaluation and who have first been informe d of, and agreed to be bound by , the terms of this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: The Recipient will safeguard and strictly control the dissemination of the Evaluati on Material and not release or disclose any Evaluation Material to any Individual, other than its Representatives and in each case only those Representatives who need to receive such information in connection with its Evaluation and who have first been informe d of, and agreed to be bound by , the terms of this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'Person' to 'Individual' in Section 3, where the agreement also defines Person, a disagreement arises. 'Person' includes both natural and legal persons (corporations, trusts, etc.), but using 'Individual' only refers to natural persons. This allows the Recipient to disclose Evaluation Material to corporations, trusts, or partnerships without breaching the agreement, contradicting the broader protection intended by using 'Person'. This contradiction is created directly in Section 3.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 3. Safeguard and Representatives: The Recipient will safeguard and strictly control the dissemination of the Evaluati on Material and not release or disclose any Evaluation Material to any Individual, other than its Representatives and in each case only those Representatives who need to receive such information in connection with its Evaluation and who have first been informe d of, and agreed to be bound by , the terms of this Agreement. The Recipient agrees to be responsible for any breach of this Agreement by any of its Representatives or by any other person to whom the Recipient ha s provided Evaluation Material and shall, up on request, forthwith provide to the Disclosing Party a list of all parties to whom Evaluation Material has been provided.
perturbation.justification: The original text uses 'Person', which, according to the explanation, includes both natural and legal persons. The changed text replaces 'Person' with 'Individual', which refers only to natural persons. This change allows the Recipient to disclose Evaluation Material to corporations, trusts, or partnerships without breaching the agreement, contradicting the broader protection originally intended. Since the Contradicted Text is identical to the Changed Text in Section 3, this further proves a contradiction exists, as the section now allows for a loophole.

perturbation.original_text: Promptly upon the Disclosing Party ’s request , the Recipient will and will cause its Representatives to: (i) return to the Disclosing Party all Evaluation Material furnished to the Recipient or its Representatives, without retaining copie s or other reproductions, reports, extracts, notes or other memoranda thereof (whether electronic, magnetic or otherwise); (ii) destroy or have destroyed all reproductions, memoranda, notes, reports, extracts, compilations, analyses and documents and all d ocuments prepared by or in the possession of the Recipient or its Representatives related to the information contained in the Evaluation Material but which does not itself constitute Evaluation Material; and (iii) provide to the Disclosing Party an officer ’s certificate that the terms and conditions of this paragraph have been complied with.
perturbation.changed_text: Promptly upon the Disclosing Party ’s request , the Recipient will and will cause its Representatives to: (i) return to the Disclosing Party all Evaluation Material furnished to the Recipient or its Representatives, without retaining copie s or other reproductions, reports, extracts, notes or other memoranda thereof (whether electronic, magnetic or otherwise); and (ii) destroy or have destroyed all reproductions, memoranda, notes, reports, extracts, compilations, analyses and documents and all d ocuments prepared by or in the possession of the Recipient or its Representatives related to the information contained in the Evaluation Material but which does not itself constitute Evaluation Material.
perturbation.explanation: Removing the obligation to 'provide to the Disclosing Party an officer’s certificate that the terms and conditions of this paragraph have been complied with' in Section 4 directly contradicts the Disclosing Party's ability to ensure compliance. This weakens the enforcement mechanism and leads to uncertainty because while the Recipient is obliged to return/destroy material, there's no verified proof of compliance, creating an in-text contradiction, making the return/destruction clause less effective and unenforceable.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 4. Return or Destruction of Evaluation Materials: The Recipient will keep a record of the location of any Evaluation Material provided to the Recipient. Promptly upon the Disclosing Party ’s request , the Recipient will and will cause its Representatives to: (i) return to the Disclosing Party all Evaluation Material furnished to the Recipient or its Representatives, without retaining copie s or other reproductions, reports, extracts, notes or other memoranda thereof (whether electronic, magnetic or otherwise); and (ii) destroy or have destroyed all reproductions, memoranda, notes, reports, extracts, compilations, analyses and documents and all d ocuments prepared by or in the possession of the Recipient or its Representatives related to the information contained in the Evaluation Material but which does not itself constitute Evaluation Material. It is understood that neither this Agreement nor the disclosure of any Evaluation Material to the Recipient should be construed as granting to the Recipient or any of its Representatives any licence or rights in respect of any part of the Evaluation Material. Furthermore, it is acknowledged that the Recipient’s computer system may automatically back -up Evaluation Material disclosed to it under the Agreement. To the extent that such computer back -up procedures create copies of the Red River Oil Inc. Confidentiality Agreement Page 3 of 8 Evaluation Material, Recipient may retain such copies in its archival o r back -up computer storage for the period it normally archives backed -up computer records, which copies shall be subject to the provision of this Agreement until the same are destroyed, and shall not be accessed by Recipient during such period of archival or back -up storage other than as might be required by this Agreement. Notwithstanding the destruction or return of the Evaluation Material, Recipient and its Representatives will continue to be bound by the obligations of confidentially and all other oblig ations hereunder during the term of this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The changed text removes the requirement for the Recipient to provide an officer's certificate confirming compliance with the terms of returning or destroying evaluation materials. The contradicted text in Section 4 outlines the process for returning or destroying these materials. The original text included a mechanism for ensuring compliance (the officer's certificate). By removing this, the changed text weakens the enforcement of the return/destruction clause outlined in the contradicted text, creating a contradiction regarding the verification and enforceability of the agreement. This aligns with the Misaligned Terminology definition, as the ability to verify compliance is a form of 'term' in the execution of the contract.

perturbation.original_text: The Recipient acknowledges and agre es that the Disclosing Party will be irreparably damaged if any provision of this Agreement is not performed by the Recipient or its Representatives in accordance with its terms and that monetary damages would not be sufficient to remedy any breach by the Recipient or its Representatives of any term or provision of this Agreement and the Recipient further agree s that the Disclosing Party shall be entitled to equitable relief, including injunction and specific performance, in the event of any breach hereof a nd in addition to any other remedy available at law or in equity.
perturbation.changed_text: The Recipient acknowledges and agre es that the Disclosing Party will be irreparably damaged if any provision of this Agreement is not performed by the Recipient or its Representatives in accordance with its terms and that monetary damages would be sufficient to remedy any breach by the Recipient or its Representatives of any term or provision of this Agreement and the Recipient further agree s that the Disclosing Party shall be entitled to equitable relief, including injunction and specific performance, in the event of any breach hereof a nd in addition to any other remedy available at law or in equity.
perturbation.explanation: Replacing 'monetary damages would not be sufficient' with 'monetary damages would be sufficient' directly contradicts the assertion that the Disclosing Party would be irreparably damaged and require equitable relief. If monetary damages are sufficient, then equitable relief like injunction and specific performance may not be warranted or granted by a court. This contradiction undermines the Disclosing Party's ability to seek immediate and decisive action in case of a breach, making the remedies section conflicting and unenforceable.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 17. Remedies for Breach: The Recipient acknowledges and agre es that the Disclosing Party will be irreparably damaged if any provision of this Agreement is not performed by the Recipient or its Representatives in accordance with its terms and that monetary damages would be sufficient to remedy any breach by the Recipient or its Representatives of any term or provision of this Agreement and the Recipient further agree s that the Disclosing Party shall be entitled to equitable relief, including injunction and specific performance, in the event of any breach hereof a nd in addition to any other remedy available at law or in equity. The Recipient further agrees to waive any requirement for the deposit of security or posting of any bond in connection with any equitable remedy.
perturbation.justification: The changed text replaces 'monetary damages would not be sufficient' with 'monetary damages would be sufficient'. This directly contradicts the assertion that the Disclosing Party will be irreparably damaged and require equitable relief, which is stated in the contradicted text, and the changed text itself. This undermines the Disclosing Party's ability to seek immediate and decisive action in case of a breach, making the remedies section conflicting and potentially unenforceable, which is a strong in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: 18. Jurisdiction: This Agreement will be gover ned by and construed in accordance with the laws of Alberta and b oth parties hereby irrevocably and unconditionally consent to and submit to the exclusive jurisdiction of the courts of Alberta for any actions, suits or proceedings arising out of the interp retation or enforcement of this Agreement (and both parties agree not to commence any action, suit or proceeding relating thereto except in such courts) and further agree that service of any process, summons, notice or document by personal delivery to its address set forth below shall be effective service of process for any action, suit or proceeding brought against the Recipient in any such court.
perturbation.changed_text: 18. Jurisdiction: This Agreement will be gover ned by and construed in accordance with the laws of British Columbia and b oth parties hereby irrevocably and unconditionally consent to and submit to the exclusive jurisdiction of the courts of Alberta for any actions, suits or proceedings arising out of the interp retation or enforcement of this Agreement (and both parties agree not to commence any action, suit or proceeding relating thereto except in such courts) and further agree that service of any process, summons, notice or document by personal delivery to its address set forth below shall be effective service of process for any action, suit or proceeding brought against the Recipient in any such court.
perturbation.explanation: Changing the governing law from 'Alberta' to 'British Columbia' while keeping the exclusive jurisdiction in the courts of 'Alberta' creates a direct contradiction. The contract is now governed by the laws of British Columbia, but any legal action must be pursued in Alberta. This creates a conflict of law issue, as Alberta courts would need to interpret and apply British Columbia law. This contradiction affects the entire section on Jurisdiction, creating significant uncertainty in legal enforcement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 18. Jurisdiction: This Agreement will be gover ned by and construed in accordance with the laws of British Columbia and b oth parties hereby irrevocably and unconditionally consent to and submit to the exclusive jurisdiction of the courts of Alberta for any actions, suits or proceedings arising out of the interp retation or enforcement of this Agreement (and both parties agree not to commence any action, suit or proceeding relating thereto except in such courts) and further agree that service of any process, summons, notice or document by personal delivery to its address set forth below shall be effective service of process for any action, suit or proceeding brought against the Recipient in any such court. Both parties hereby irrevocably and unconditionally waive any objection to the laying of venue of any action, suit or proceeding arising out of this Agreement or the matters contemplated hereby in the courts of Alberta and hereby further irrevocably and unconditionally waive and agree not to plead or claim in any such court that any such action, suit or proceedin g so brought has been brought in an inconvenient forum.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies the governing law from 'Alberta' to 'British Columbia' while maintaining the exclusive jurisdiction in the courts of 'Alberta'. This creates a contradiction because the agreement is now governed by British Columbia law, but legal actions must be pursued in Alberta courts, which would need to interpret and apply British Columbia law. The contradicted text is the same section with the same contradiction. Therefore, the change does create a direct conflict as the courts of Alberta will now have to operate under the laws of British Columbia, which goes against standard procedure.
